author: @sylverkat9488
body:
Thank you for putting this together. I was unaware of the token usage limits going from GPT to Claude, but learned them very quickly. I did find an extension that helped me keep a close eye on my token usage in chat, called Claude Counter, and it plugs into Chrome. I'm sure there are others, but it's taught me how to use the tokens. I've also found that keeping the chats short helps, too, start a new one around 30k tokens. You can ask Claude to make a summary for you that will seamlessly start a new chat with the same context, and it will happily write that summary to use as a prompt for the next one. 30k seems to be the point where it starts losing detail too, so it's a good cutoff. 

If you're front loading a lot of data it will be a short chat - yes - but since Claude uses the whole chat every time it answers you, it helps to keep them short. Hopefully this helps others too.
